paper.title: Connect the Dots: Training LLMs for Long-Lifecycle Agents with Cross-Domain Generalization Via Reinforcement Learning

research_equation.formulation: How to train LLMs to elicit a meta-capability for long-lifecycle agentic deployment where the agent must accumulate knowledge about an environment and transfer it across different tasks?

components.input: LLM (pre-trained), collection of environments with multiple tasks per environment
components.process: End-to-end RL with interleaved solve-task and update-context episodes, GRPO-style algorithm with fine-grained credit assignment across episodes
components.output: LLM with CoD meta-capability that can self-improve during deployment by updating its context
components.constraints: Clean reward signals (preferably outcome rewards), information-theoretic limits on per-task success without context, environment diversity for generalization

first_pass.title: What: CoD meta-capability for LLM-based agents
highlights: LLMs today lack the ability to accumulate and transfer knowledge across tasks during long-lifecycle deployment
Standard task-by-task RL trains each task from scratch, misaligned with CoD
Framework with CoD-Deploy (deployment abstraction) and CoD-Train (RL training with matching rollout pattern)
Proof-of-concept with Qwen3-8B on FrozenLake-Obscure, Alchemy-Random, TerminalSimulator
Key empirical result: position 3 success rate jumps from 28% to 76% after CoD-Train
contributions: General CoD framework for LLM training with long rollout sequences
GRPO-style RL algorithm with fine-grained credit assignment across solve/update episodes
Dedicated environments with information-theoretic gaps for training/evaluation
Evidence of cross-domain and Ralph-loop OOD generalization
limitations: Small-scale proof-of-concept (8B model, short 4/8 task sequences)

second_pass.title: How: RL algorithm design and environment engineering
details: GRPO-style algorithm adapted for multi-episode trajectories with per-episode rewards
Return = mean of rewards-to-go (current + future solve-task rewards)
Baseline = per-position group mean across parallel rollout trajectories
Adaptive RED-Weight augmentation to stabilize training: token-level re-weighting via temperature bisection when mean advantage is negative
FrozenLake-Obscure: abstract action symbols with random mappings → information-theoretic gap
Alchemy-Random: random element/recipe sets → exploration needed across episodes
TerminalSimulator: cross-domain evaluation with terminal command tasks

third_pass.title: What's missing / what's next
gaps: Scalability to larger models (70B+), longer sequences (hundreds of tasks), and more diverse environments
Theoretical grounding for the adaptive RED-Weight heuristic
More principled return calculation (discounting/sliding windows for long sequences)
Integration with standard LLM post-training pipelines
Grounded comparison with baselines (SFT, behavioral cloning)
next_steps: Scale up environment diversity for CoD-Train to improve generalization
Validate on real-world scenarios (personal assistant, coding agent)
Design more principled RL algorithm without heuristic augmentations
Explore flexible rollout patterns beyond current solve→update interleaving
Integrate into LLM post-training as sequential stage or via model merging

challenge: credit_assignment_across_epis.description: Long state-action sequences containing multiple solve-task and update-context episodes, each possibly multi-turn with many tokens
challenge: credit_assignment_across_epis.module: GRPO-style algorithm with rewards-to-go returns, per-position group baselines, adaptive RED-Weight
challenge: credit_assignment_across_epis.remaining_issues: Theoretical grounding missing; baseline not fully comparable across trajectories with different context

challenge: environment_design.description: Need environments that incentivize context-updating rather than per-task solving from scratch
challenge: environment_design.module: FrozenLake-Obscure (info-theoretic limits from hidden action mapping), Alchemy-Random (hidden recipes)
challenge: environment_design.remaining_issues: Only 3 environments; need broader and more realistic environments

challenge: out_of_distribution_generaliz.description: The elicited CoD meta-capability must transfer across domains, settings, and harder instances
challenge: out_of_distribution_generaliz.module: Multi-environment CoD-Train (Setting B), evaluation on unseen domains and Ralph-loop
challenge: out_of_distribution_generaliz.remaining_issues: TerminalSimulator results ambiguous; wider OOD validation needed

challenge: training_stability.description: GRPO-style algorithms exhibit instability on certain domains (Alchemy-Random)
challenge: training_stability.module: Adaptive RED-Weight augmentation with temperature bisection
challenge: training_stability.remaining_issues: Hacky heuristic; theoretically grounded method desirable

challenge: context_mechanism.description: How to represent and update cross-episode context effectively
challenge: context_mechanism.module: Single 'hint' text appended to system prompt
challenge: context_mechanism.remaining_issues: Simplistic; needs generalization to persistent memory banks, Markdown skills files
